Will Smith, Jr.: My name is Will.
I live here in South Carolina.

Thank you.
Tonight's guest is Will.
Will, welcome to the show.

Happy to be here.
What's great having you.
Thank you for your time.
Will, please give us a brief bio on yourself.
Will Smith, Jr.: My name is Will.
I live here in South Carolina.
I'm originally from Southern California.
I am 50 years old and I'm an avid outdoorsman, adventurous kind of person who likes to go
enjoy things that are outside and grew up just enjoying life, everything life has to deal with.
That must have been one heck of a culture shock, moving from Southern Cal all the way over to
South Carolina. Wow. It was. It was a quick adjustment, but it was different from the
hustle and bustle of LA, that's for sure. Oh, I'm sure it was an adjustment. Yeah,
I can only imagine.
Will, you grew up in a family that believed in cryptids.
Please tell us more about that.
Well, yeah, I grew up in the 70s, early 70s.
I was a young kid, and we always watched TV as a family.
It was back before remote controls were popular.
The kids were the remote controls.
We'd watch stuff like In Search Of, and my mom would always bring up certain topics about Bigfoot, for example,
or Loch Ness Monster or something like that.
And she would go into detail about certain things.
When she was a little girl living out in Indiana and Arizona and things like that,
where she would say, yeah, I believe in these things.
I don't think my mom's ever seen one.
She's not with us no more.
But I'm not sure if she ever actually saw a cryptid, but she was a believer in cryptids.
And my dad, I think he was to an extent, but I think he mostly tolerated my mom's stories and whatnot.
Well, how neat is that, though, still to have your mom being into cryptids like that?
That's great.
It was interesting.
I mean, it scared the scared the garbage out of us as kids.
You know, it was kind of those things like if you don't do this and something's going to get you and then she'd go into a story, you know.
But it was she kept us on our toes.
Let's just put it that way.
We were an avid camping family, so we'd be out in the woods a lot and sometimes
she'd bring up Bigfoot and whatnot.
And it was interesting, interesting to say the least.
So I kind of started my, my culture with cryptids.
I didn't know what the word cryptid was back then, but you know, I knew what the
elusive Bigfoot was because of, you know, in search of, and my mom talking about it
and then campsite stories and whatnot.
So it was a big deal.
It was always in the back of my mind as a kid growing up.
well like i said how cool is that that's great how much trouble did you have finding out what
it was that you saw the night of your encounter um not too much to be honest with you it wasn't
something that was ever on my radar i really never heard of dog man before growing up especially on
the west coast i mean i don't think it was something that people talked about you know
werewolf style cryptids. For me, it was always Bigfoot. So to hear about it when I came out here

in Mosul which we talk about a little bit it was just an eye experience It was like a floodgate opened up and I just started learning more and more about certain things that I didn know really existed and possibilities of existing So it was intriguing to say the least
Oh, I'm sure it was.
Yeah, there's life before you find out about dogmen and definitely life after.
Yeah, I mean, I always thought it was just Bigfoot and Loch Ness Monster and things like that.
I didn't really think of like other dimension stuff that could be out there, you know, and find out there's even Mothman and all those kind of things in West Virginia.
And I've been up there many times and heard the stories now that I've lived on this side of the country.
And it just is an eye opening experience.
Well, if you don't know, you don't know, but I'm glad you know now.
Were you able to tell your mom about your encounter before she passed?
I did, actually.
It was, I think it took me a couple of days to get the courage to do it.
I remember calling her and mentioning it to her and she was, I don't think she knew about
this particular cryptid, at least not the way I saw it, but she's like, yeah, that's,
that's interesting.
And then the fact that I live out here in an area that's Indian related, it started
making her think about it more and more.
We talked about it for a little while about the experience.

And she believed me, which was really nice to have someone in my family actually, you know, give me the satisfaction of actually listening and believing in what I was saying and not think I was crazy about it.
So it was pretty cool.
It was nice to have my mom on my side.
Oh, I'm sure it was.
Did you get the impression when you told her about your encounter that she almost got a little excited?
Because instead of being in books and on TV, now my son has actually had an encounter with a cryptid.
yeah i would say she was pretty excited i think it got her juices flowing so to speak where she
was really thinking about it more and more about what was going on and the experience that i had
and that it was actually something like you said it wasn't in a book it was physically something
you can almost touch you know so it was it was kind of like i think maybe a validation maybe for
because now it's like like i said i don't know if she actually ever had an experience with the
Bigfoot or encrypted, but now she can actually say, yeah, it's my son's had it.
And maybe she never even shared it.
She can actually say, yeah.
And now I know that this is true because my son doesn't lie to me, which I never lied
Good man.
And also I'm sure she wore that like a badge of honor.
She was a good woman.
Well, considering how nice you are, I'm sure she was.
Thank you.
You're welcome.
Would you say your encounter was the most frightening experience you've had in your

And if not, what was?
I would say it was weird.
It was unexpected.
So I don't know if frightening is a good word for it, but it was unexpected.
I've had other situations where, and I had to think about this more and more after we

was just unexpected and it something that came at me that I had no control over is what kind of

I had a situation back in California that I was out on a ride at night with some friends of mine
we were on a group patrol and uh i swear i thought something like we were riding on in the dark
with our lights so you're on a single track trail so you're probably about a little over a foot and
a half to two feet size trail we're riding our bikes i was in the back of this three-person group
on these mountain bikes it was probably around 12 o'clock at night and we were climbing this ridge
and one side was a complete drop off about 30 feet into some rocks and you didn't really you
couldn't see it because it was dark on the other side on the right side of me there was like these
trees and i remember this limb was pulled back and then thrown at me like on a tree a sapling type
tree and it hit me in the face and i flew off my bike my bike and me went off the edge of the cliff
into the dark and i was more afraid of that than this dogman encounter that i just had
a couple years ago.
Because that I had no control of.
I had no idea it was happening.
I thought maybe it was vertigo.
I don't know what it could have been,
but I was in an area that wasn't,
it was populated,
but it wasn't populated at night
and I was out of control
and there's no reason for me.
I was a great bike handler.
I should never have fallen off my bike
the way I did.
And that freaked me out.
Well, I bet it did.
That branch was definitely pulled back
and then let go I believe it was because there was no wind that night that we were riding i couldn tell you what time of the year it was it probably summertime and uh there no reason why that
that limb was my buddies didn't hit it they were in front of me i was only like a bike length behind

mountains for the national park system.
And I was carrying medical, which is pretty funny because I was the one with
the medical pack and the radio.
And here I am flying 30 feet into a ditch in the rocks, like a cliff in the middle

so scared in my life because I don't know how that tree hit, like it just like swung
back and slapped me in the face, knocked me right off my mountain bike.
Wow, that is rough. Were you hurt?
Yeah, I was pretty banged up. We were about an hour into our tour, our ride, and I think I hurt the left side of my ribs. Got hit pretty bad.
I kind of rolled onto my back where my medical pack was, so that cushioned a lot of the fall, but my mountain bike fell on top of me.
and then my buddies had to try to climb down and pull me you know get me out of that little canyon
and i didn't want them to call in for anybody to like medical to come get me because we were

and they got me back up there got back on the bike we finished our tour a couple hours later
and that was it but i to this day i don't know why that branch was flung back at me and smacked
me in the face. But I remember that like it was yesterday. It was crazy.
Sure does make you wonder what did that then?
I don't know. I know that there was mountain lions in the area. We had coyotes.
No bear that I know of in that area. I don't know. It was just a weird, weird thing that happened.
Yeah, that's a good way to sum it up. It kind of reminds me of that one scene from the movie

Well, Corey Haim winds up taking in the dog,
and since he has a dog with him and his family,
then the XCOM comes after him.
And there's a scene in the movie where one of his buddies
is out in the woods riding with his friends.
Well, the boys are riding, and all of a sudden,
that XCOM comes after them because Corey had been with them,
and this branch just gets pulled back and flung right into this kid's face
and knocks him off the bike and it jumps on the kid and it kills him.
But when you talked about what happened,
it kind of took me back to that one scene in the movie and I thought, wow, that's rough.
Well, I'm glad nothing tried to kill me, just the rocks.
Yeah, me too.
Me too.
If you've had a dogman encounter and would like to speak with me about it,
whether in private or on the show,
please go to dogmeanencounters.com and submit a report.
If you've had a Bigfoot sighting and would like to be a guest on one of my two Bigfoot shows,
please go to mybigfootsighting.com.
All right, Will, please tell us about your encounter now.
Give us every last detail that comes to mind.
All right, I will.
Well, it was, I think, in around 2020, just before the pandemic area timeframe.
I went over to a buddy's house. It was probably October-ish, late October, I believe. I went to
a friend's house who lived about six miles away from my house. I lived on, just to preface this,
I lived on Lake Hartwell in the upstate. They call it the Golden Triangle area. It's right on the
border of Georgia. So Livonia is the next city next to where I lived in. I lived in the town
called Airplay. And so I live right there on the lake. So across the lake, which I lived on,
I could see Georgia every day. It was just right across the lake. There was a state line.
Well, my buddy, he lived about six miles away and he wanted, we were, I was going to his house to
help him work on some motorcycles. Cause that's something we do. We ride Harleys. And so I went
to his house. It was probably around four o'clock, five o'clock ish. And we were working until pretty
late. It was probably about 11 o'clock. So I had dinner at his house with his wife and
just had a good time just relaxing and being guys working on, you know, car stuff and motorcycle stuff. And
I got to the point where I was like Okay I going to go home Well I didn ride my motorcycle to this house which is a good thing in my opinion I drove my little convertible and I had a Fiat 500 Abarth
So the roof was completely cloth, and the car's not that big.
It's a pretty small car, but this particular version of the 500 was really loud.
It had open mufflers, and it was a little tuned differently.
It was faster than the regular 500s that were out there from Fiat.
anyway so I was driving home probably around 11 30 at night and I decided to take the long way
which takes me down some scenic back country roads because I didn't really want to get home
I just kind of wanted to play around and just drive the car and enjoy the night it was a nice
cool evening I think it was kind of clear so it was nice but I didn't put the top down I left the
top up in the car and I'm going down these farm areas in the back area with a lot of trees
South Carolina is known for their trees and their vegetation here.
It's always green year-round.
And as I'm driving, I'm heading out towards our interstate, which is Interstate 85.
And what I was going to do is I was going to go all the way from his house to the interstate to the back way,
get on 85, and then get off the last exit, which is the exit where I lived off of,
which is Highway 11 or Exit 1 in South Carolina, and then head back to my house.
So it's taking a longer route.
home. So I was driving that way. I got on the interstate, hooked onto the Highway 11,
which is known as the Cherokee Highway. And it goes past Hartwell State Park,
which is a camping ground slash boat landing right there across from Georgia State Line.
And so I was just passing that as I got on Highway 11,
driving my car. I'm going probably 70 miles an hour.
visibility was it was dark but it was very clear so you can see pretty far up and i had all my
lights on my car so i had fog lights and all this other stuff set up at nighttime so i can really
see where i was going my biggest fear was a deer was going to run out in front of me so i wanted
to make sure i could see anything in front of me before i made that transition home so i didn't
hit anything because a lot of people are known to hit deer in this area especially around that
time at night. They like to cross the highway 11 a lot. So I'm driving, kind of minding my own
business, listening to some music and just kind of chilling and thinking about what me and my buddy
did, you know, working on his bike so we can get ready for a run this weekend that we were, I think
it was a Thursday night that this happened. So we were getting ready for a run on Saturday.
We're all going to get together and ride our motorcycles. So as I'm driving, I kind of noticed
something on the right side of the road on the side of the road it wasn't in the road but it
was like off the shoulder probably about i don't know 100 yards in front of me just barely where
my lights were hitting at the time and it was a lot of movement and i couldn't really tell what
it was i thought maybe it was a deer i didn't think anything of it i'm from california so i
didn't really think about slowing down i just kind of was mesmerized by what i was looking at
and as I'm driving closer and closer whatever it was noticed my car I think it heard it
and my car is just loud it makes a nice rumble and back back pops and all this kind of stuff
with the vehicle it poked its head up looked at me and then started coming towards me and it
started galloping and I thought that's really odd I've never seen an animal just kind of come
towards a moving vehicle. I've seen him go across the road and disappear, but never charge something.
And it started picking up at speed. As I'm looking at it, I could tell that it was kind of like a dog
style animal, but it looked so much bigger than any dog I've ever seen in my life. And I'm thinking
back to like when I was doing stuff in California in the woods and I remember coyotes and how thin
and sickly they look. And I was looking at the structure of the ears. I could tell that it was
kind of like a coyote slash wolf we don't have wolves in this area south carolina so it really
threw me for a loop i was kind of freaking out because i wasn't you know for one i'm driving
this little car and i'm driving a convertible and i didn't want to hit it so i kept the speed
up around 70 miles an hour and it was kept getting closer and closer to me i could see the eyes
glowing like a yellowish tent so I knew it was looking at me as it was coming towards me I did
notice that the hair on this animal was like a silver white so it was really brilliant with the
concrete background and the dark behind it so I could really see a good look at this thing and I
noticed that it was almost as wide if not as wide as my car my car is only about a little over five
feet wide. It's not very wide. It's a
really narrow car. And I could tell that this thing was probably bigger than my car. And that's
when I got freaked out a little bit or more concerned at what was coming at me. This thing
was coming quick. It had a big, wide shoulders behind the head. It had pointy ears. I didn't
see any teeth, but I wasn't really paying attention to teeth, but I was paying more
attention to the size of this animal and as it was coming at me. And it was galloping towards
me at a pretty fast rate and my only concern at this point was okay it's going to get off the
road it's going to ditch to the left of its body and go back into the woods and it never did it
it actually got more into the side of the road in line with my car than i thought it was going to i
thought it was just going to go across the road or was going to ditch back into the woods this is a
heavily wooded area on the driver's side you've got lake hartwell which is a very big lake a lot
openings for animals to come through there we got a lot of deer and things like that we even have
bear up in this area a lot of coyote type animals a lot of foxes armadillos things like that but
nothing this like this i for a second i questioned that it could be like a some kind of wolfhound
type thing a domesticated dog but it it just was so big is there's just no way it was somebody's pet
and then it started making me think as it was coming closer to me could this animal be rabid
it could it have rabies and that's it sees me as a threat and wants to attack me so the movie
Kunjo came to my head thinking of that movie Kunjo how Kunjo was attacking that lady in her car
and I'm like this just this isn't good so it was freaking me out um as I got closer and closer to
this creature, I got to a point where I said, okay, it's not going to move. I don't have much
in my car to protect me, but my handgun that I keep in there at all times, which is only a nine
millimeter, I don't think this thing would have done anything to it. And I hear stories about
people talking about, I had a gun on me and I was in the woods and, you know, I don't think it would
have done anything. I just think that was silly to hear. I mean, it would do something, but I got
that impression as I looked at this creature coming towards me, even my car wouldn't have
done much to it. It probably would have dented the garbage out of my car, but then I'd be in
trouble and it'd be in the car with me. And I didn't want that, especially with the roof that
I had. It was just a, you know, open top roof and just, it would fall through and I'd be sitting
there next to this thing or it'd be on top of me. So I felt really insecure about that. So there's
The only option I had was to continue to play chicken with this animal.
And by the time I got to a point where I knew I can swerve to the left onto oncoming traffic,
which there's no traffic at night on this road, it was just me and this animal,
that was my safest and best bet to do.
So what I did is once it got to about 15 feet or so near me and I could see the massive size of it,
it was tall.
It was big, tall, and just bulky.
that's when I made the move to swerve over to the left and then swerve directly back to the right
right away so I got back in my lane as I did that this animal came close to my right side of my car
I could tell that it was taller than my window on my passenger side and it was a real fluffy
clean wasn't dirty fur of white and silver and I couldn't see the head after that I could barely
I couldn't see the back of this animal as it went past me.
It was that tall.
And as I got back into the other lane where I was originally going,
I could see in the taillights that it was behind me,
and it stopped, slowed down, and then went into the woods.
Now, at that point where I saw it,
this was just after Hartwell State Park entrance that all this took place.
There's an open area on the right.
It's an open field.
Some people usually go out there and hunt.
So there's a lot of game animal out there.
so it's possible that this thing was hunting deer at the time or it found roadkill on the side of
the road and i just drove up because it was on the side of the road when i first saw this thing
and maybe i just interrupted it and it freaked out and wanted me out of its area for it to

and I'm like oh my gosh I'm not the only one who's had the same experience this is crazy
and I remember calling my buddy who I was with the night before and his office wasn't too far

saw this thing me and my boss we were driving in our big ford f-250 that's pretty jacked up
it's an off-road vehicle he said we saw something that matches the description and it was something

that made no sense to him but he wasn't the kind of guy who's going to go start talking about

but they remember seeing it on the side of the road going into the bushes and it was massive
he said it would fill the back of his truck it was so big so that kind of validated how i felt
I wasn't imagining this situation.
I wasn't tired when I was driving home from his house that night that we actually saw the same creature, the same color, same everything, same build.
His was just more towards dusk time.
It was still light out.
Mine was just at night, and I saw it with my headlights on my car.
And to this day, I still drive that road.
I moved away for a while, and I just recently moved back to this area.
I don't live exactly in that same area, but I'm only about six miles away from it.
And I'll still go out there and drive that same route looking for this creature to see if it's still out there.

Well, I'm more prepared now than I was before.
I'll just put it that way, I guess.
It doesn't frighten me.
It intrigues me.

I'm not, you know, they call you a believer if you believe in Bigfoot, but you're a knower if you've seen it.
Well, I've seen this creature, and it doesn't match any creature that's ever come across my path before.

I know it's there.

And there's some strange stuff that goes on here in this area in South Carolina that I

But I know that Dog Man was out there.
I mean, I'm not the only one who's seen this one.
Well, like you said, you're not a believer now.
You're a knower.
You know they out there I do And it does make me think back in the past where I been in different areas across this country doing certain stuff that I done in the past like the mountain bike thing

It makes me wonder what was around me when I was out there doing these things.
I've been around a lot of situations where I've heard things that didn't make any sense.

that now makes me kind of wonder when I'm out in the woods, I'm out hunting or something like that,
or even ride my motorcycle, what's around me, what's watching me?
And it just keeps me more on my guard, more so than I ever been before.
It's not a comforting thought, but while you've been out hunting and doing other things like that, they've probably been watching you more times than you'd ever believe.
I believe that, yeah. Animals are smarter than we are sometimes when it comes to being in their territory. We're visitors.
Oh, no doubt. Yeah, we definitely are.
Were you upset with Brad for not telling you about his encounter before you told him about having yours?
No, not at all. It was actually kind of... It was neat. It was nice. I actually was going
through his texts before our interview here. I went back to the very beginning of when I first
met him and we started texting each other, becoming friends and building that relationship

in what he saw than just telling somebody on the street just the validation of him filling
in some of the blanks before i even mentioned them to him like the color the size the mannerism
of this creature made me feel so much more closer to him about it.
I just wish he was still with us today.
No, I totally understand.
And I'm so sorry to hear that he passed.
Sounds like he was a great guy.
And I think you were lucky to have him as a friend, just like he was lucky to have you.
It was just, if you knew Brad, you would know that he's not the kind of person that would

He never, if he didn't see it, he didn't see it.
He wouldn't say anything.
Before I met him, this is how he knew me.
I came and said, hey, I hear you're an avid hunter.
This is before we became really close.
I'm like, you're an avid hunter.
Have you seen anything strange in the woods?
I mean, I know your pattern.
You're out there before the sun comes up and hours after the sun goes down.
And you're usually in the middle of places that no one even goes out to.
You've had to have seen something.
And he's like, no, I've never seen a thing.
Nothing that freaks me out.
I'm like, okay.
And I believe you.
And then after just talking to him about the situation and hearing his, his side of it
